title: Native ordered categorical model with minimal manual target-style preprocessing
summary: Your best code still relies on many hand-built frequency and median features over high-cardinality identifiers.
rationale: Your best code still relies on many hand-built frequency and median features over high-cardinality identifiers. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and categorical combinations are designed to reduce prediction shift and leakage from categorical statistics, which is especially relevant with Driver and Race identities across seasons.
implementation_hint: Test a lean feature set with raw categoricals kept as strings: Driver, Race, Compound, Year, Stint, plus a small number of strong numerics and physically motivated interactions. Let CatBoost build ordered CTR-style combinations; compare against the handcrafted baseline under the same folds.
expected_effect: Could recover signal from high-cardinality identity interactions that manual frequency encoding misses while reducing overfit from dataset-level aggregates.
risk: If the competition test distribution has many unseen drivers/races or if folds are not time/group safe, gains can vanish or turn into misleading CV improvements.
